fields.Name: NetSuite
fields.Description: Oracle NetSuite ERP/CRM connector: SuiteTalk REST Record Service (CRUD) + SuiteQL reads across standard and custom record types; incremental via lastModifiedDate; OAuth 1.0a TBA / OAuth 2.0; per-account host; runtime metadata-catalog discovery.

universalPK.note: Every NetSuite REST record carries a numeric 'id' field as its internal system primary key. It appears as an integer in GET responses (e.g. '107' or '42') and is used as the path parameter in all record operations: GET /services/rest/record/v1/{recordType}/{id}. Consistent across all standard and custom record types. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide 'Getting a Record Instance'; Collection Paging example responses showing 'id' field on every item; subrecord example GET /services/rest/record/v1/customer/42/addressbook/24/addressbookaddress. Docs URL: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_1540810951.html (Listing All Record Instances showing items[].id) and https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_156414087576.html (Collection Paging showing id on every item).

Configuration.HostTemplateNote: NetSuite uses per-account subdomains for all REST calls. The accountId is a per-connection runtime value (e.g. '1234567' for production or '1234567_SB1' for sandbox) supplied in the credential. The connector builds the base URL at runtime as 'https://{accountId}.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com'. This is NOT a baked-in constant. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide request examples throughout: GET https://demo123.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/record/v1/customer (demo account 'demo123'). Postman tutorial notes: 'You can download the REST API Postman environment template and collection of sample requests from the SuiteTalk tools download page at https://{accountID}.app.netsuite.com/...'. Async job response Location header example: 'https://demo123.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/async/v1/job/{id}'. Docs URL: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_1540391670.html (Overview) and https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_1544794192.html (Postman chapter).
Configuration.AuthFlowNote: NetSuite TBA (Token-Based Authentication) uses OAuth 1.0a-style four-part signing: ConsumerKey, ConsumerSecret, TokenID, TokenSecret — all from the Integration Record + Access Token. This is the currently implemented credential type (NetSuite TBA, ID A72945D2-9E38-429D-904D-F3ACCE17CBAB). The connector signs each request with an Authorization header containing OAuth realm, oauth_consumer_key, oauth_token, oauth_signature_method, oauth_timestamp, oauth_nonce, and oauth_signature. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide Setting Up Authentication page (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_0627022005.html): 'NetSuite supports two mechanisms: OAuth 2.0 and token-based authentication (TBA), robust, industry standard-based mechanisms. These authentication mechanisms enable client applications to use a token to access NetSuite through APIs, eliminating the need for integrations to store user credentials. REST web services do not support login through user credentials.' Also: 'Note: OAuth 2.0 is the preferred authentication method. You should consider using OAuth 2.0 instead of TBA whenever possible.'
Configuration.AuthFlowAlternateNote: NetSuite also supports OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow. Requires creating an Integration Record and obtaining client_id + client_secret. Oracle states OAuth 2.0 is the PREFERRED method but is not the currently implemented credential type for this connector. Setting Up OAuth 2.0 Authentication for REST Web Services: 'To set up OAuth 2.0, see Getting Started with OAuth 2.0. To be able to use REST web services with OAuth 2.0, you're required to create an application using an integration record.' Source: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_0627022005.html
Configuration.AuthHeaderPattern: Authorization: OAuth realm="{accountId}", oauth_consumer_key="{ConsumerKey}", oauth_token="{TokenID}", oauth_signature_method="HMAC-SHA256", oauth_timestamp="{ts}", oauth_nonce="{nonce}", oauth_version="1.0", oauth_signature="{sig}"
Configuration.AuthHeaderPatternNote: NetSuite TBA uses OAuth 1.0a Authorization header with realm set to the account ID. The signature is HMAC-SHA256 over the base string per OAuth 1.0 spec. For OAuth 2.0, the pattern is the standard 'Authorization: Bearer <access_token>'. The connector implements TBA signing. Source: Implied from netsuite-tba.schema.json credential structure (ConsumerKey, ConsumerSecret, TokenID, TokenSecret) and OAuth 1.0a/TBA documentation. EvidenceStrength: ImpliedFromExample (the schema fields and NetSuite TBA documentation together imply this header shape).
Configuration.RecordServiceBasePathNote: All CRUD operations on NetSuite records go through /services/rest/record/v1/{recordType} (list/create) and /services/rest/record/v1/{recordType}/{id} (read/update/delete/upsert). Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide, chapter 'Working with Records' and examples throughout: GET https://demo123.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/record/v1/customer, POST /services/rest/record/v1/salesorder, PATCH /services/rest/record/v1/salesorder/1, etc. Docs: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_1540811107.html
Configuration.SuiteQLPathNote: SuiteQL queries (SQL-92-based) are executed via POST to /services/rest/query/v1/suiteql with a JSON body {q: '<SQL query>'} and the required header Prefer: transient. Paging: ?limit=N&offset=N. Max 100,000 results via REST (use SuiteAnalytics Connect ODBC for more). Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide 'Executing SuiteQL Queries Through REST Web Services' (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_157909186990.html): 'You can execute SuiteQL queries through REST web services by sending a POST request to the suiteql resource... POST https://demo123.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/query/v1/suiteql?limit=10 offset=10 Prefer: transient {"q": "SELECT email, COUNT(*) as count FROM transaction GROUP BY email"}'
Configuration.MetadataCatalogPathNote: The metadata catalog endpoint lists all available record types (GET) or their schemas in OpenAPI 3.0 (Accept: application/swagger+json) or JSON Schema (Accept: application/schema+json). A per-record-type schema is at /services/rest/record/v1/metadata-catalog/{recordType}. The catalog is per-user and shows standard + custom records/fields. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide 'Getting Metadata' (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_1540810174.html): 'The endpoint for getting the metadata schema for all exposed records is http://demo123.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/record/v1/metadata-catalog ... GET http://demo123.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/record/v1/metadata-catalog'. Working with Resource Metadata: 'The metadata catalog defines all available records, their fields, available values (for example, for enum fields), sublists and subrecords (both standard and custom ones), and their various properties. As new customizations are added to the system, they appear in the metadata catalog as well.'
Configuration.DiscoveryIsAuthoritativeNote: The /services/rest/record/v1/metadata-catalog endpoint returns the complete gamut of records accessible to the authenticated user (standard + all custom). The docs state: 'As new customizations are added to the system, they appear in the metadata catalog as well.' and 'This dynamic NetSuite model reflects the customizations and features enabled or disabled in your account.' This implies the catalog IS authoritative for the credentialed user context — if a record type no longer appears it has been genuinely removed/disabled. However, this requires runtime verification with real credentials (credential-gated) before committing DiscoveryIsAuthoritative=true. Gate: true if the connector's DiscoverObjects returns a non-zero count that matches what the account admin sees in Setup > Records Catalog. Source: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_1540810174.html + https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_159367781370.html.
Configuration.APIVersioningStrategyNote: API version is embedded in the URL path as /services/rest/record/v1/ and /services/rest/query/v1/. Currently at version v1 throughout. Source: All URL examples in SuiteTalk REST API Guide use /v1/ in paths. E.g.: GET https://demo123.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/record/v1/customer; POST https://demo123.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/query/v1/suiteql. Docs: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_1540391670.html

PaginationDefaults.description: NetSuite REST uses offset/limit pagination for both record collection listings and SuiteQL results. Default page size is 1000 records. Max 1000 pages (max 1,000,000 total results with SuiteAnalytics Connect; 100,000 without). The offset must be divisible by the limit. HATEOAS 'next', 'prev', 'first', 'last' links are included in the response. Response includes 'hasMore', 'count', 'totalResults', 'offset' fields.
PaginationDefaults.constraint: offset must be divisible by limit
PaginationDefaults.source: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_156414087576.html — 'Lists of record instances and the results of saved analytics workbook searches are returned in one or more pages. The results are displayed on multiple pages, with the default setting of 1000 results per page on a maximum of 1000 pages... You can only set offset and limit values in a way that the offset is divisible by the limit.'

Configuration.IncrementalSyncDescription: NetSuite does not have a native incremental cursor endpoint (no /updated or /changes endpoint like Salesforce). Incremental sync is implemented via SuiteQL with a lastModifiedDate filter: 'SELECT * FROM {recordType} WHERE lastModifiedDate > {watermark} ORDER BY lastModifiedDate'. The serverTime endpoint (GET /services/rest/system/v1/serverTime) returns the NetSuite server time in UTC for initializing/anchoring the watermark — the docs explicitly note this use case: 'To synchronize new or changed customers records, you can search customers and filter by lastModifiedDate using the value returned in serverTime.' Not all record types expose lastModifiedDate — per-record availability must be verified in the metadata catalog or Records Catalog. Source: serverTime operation docs (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_0528010158.html): 'To synchronize new or changed customers records, you can search customers and filter by lastModifiedDate using the value returned in serverTime. This makes your synchronization process independent of your client time...' and SuiteQL docs (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_157909186990.html).
Configuration.IncrementalWatermarkFieldNote: The lastModifiedDate system field tracks when a record was last changed. Used as the SuiteQL watermark field for incremental sync. Not all record types expose this field — check per-type in the Records Catalog. Source: serverTime operation docs (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_0528010158.html): explicit use case of 'filter by lastModifiedDate using the value returned in serverTime'. EvidenceStrength: ExplicitStatement.
Configuration.ServerTimeEndpointNote: Returns the NetSuite server time in UTC (e.g. {"serverTime": "2025-03-26T16:21:00.000Z"}). Used to seed the initial watermark for incremental sync. Source: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_0528010158.html
Configuration.GovernanceLimitsNote: Returns per-integration concurrency limit: accountConcurrencyLimit, accountUnallocatedConcurrencyLimit, and (when integration-specific) integrationConcurrencyLimit. Requires administrator role. The connector should call this at startup to discover its actual concurrency budget. Source: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_0625010817.html
Configuration.RateLimitModelNote: NetSuite does NOT use a requests-per-second or requests-per-minute rate limit. Instead it enforces a CONCURRENT REQUEST LIMIT per account, determined by the service tier and SuiteCloud Plus licenses. Exceeding the limit returns HTTP 429 with o:errorCode CONCURRENCY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED. There is no token-bucket or rolling-window limit to respect; the constraint is on in-flight simultaneous connections. The error handling docs explicitly describe the 429 response. Standard tier (no SuiteCloud Plus): 5 concurrent connections. Premium (0 licenses): 15. Enterprise (0): 20. Ultimate (0): 20. Each SuiteCloud Plus license adds 10 to the concurrency limit (up to the tier maximum). Source: SuiteCloud Plus Settings (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_N259503.html) and Error Handling docs (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_156570709583.html): 'Concurrent request limit exceeded. Request blocked.' HTTP 429 with CONCURRENCY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED.
Configuration.BatchMaxRequestCountNote: BatchMaxRequestCount is null because NetSuite uses a concurrency-based governance model (concurrent connections), not a requests-per-second/minute limit. The connector should implement concurrency control via the governanceLimits endpoint response rather than a fixed batch count. The engine's BatchMaxRequestCount slot does not model concurrency limits — use the Configuration.RateLimitModel and ConcurrencyLimitByTier fields to drive the connector's concurrency strategy.
Configuration.WebhooksNote: NetSuite SuiteTalk REST Web Services does not expose traditional inbound webhooks. The documented integration patterns are pull-based (polling via SuiteQL or REST record lists). NetSuite does offer SuiteScript-based outbound notifications and Saved Search email alerts at the platform level, but these are not REST webhook endpoints and are out of scope for this connector. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide covers no webhook subscription endpoints. EvidenceStrength: ImpliedFromExample (webhooks are simply absent from the REST guide's feature list; the guide's feature table covers CRUD, metadata, SuiteQL, datasets, async — no webhooks).
Configuration.BulkOperationsNote: NetSuite SuiteTalk REST does not expose a batch/bulk mutation API equivalent to Salesforce's Composite Batch or Bulk API 2.0. Individual record CRUD is per-record. Asynchronous processing (Prefer: respond-async header) is available for individual long-running requests but is not a batch endpoint. SuiteQL queries can return up to 100,000 records in paginated form. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide. The 'REST Web Services Operations' page lists: Attach/Detach, Batch (attach/detach operations only), Create-form, GovernanceLimits, selectOptions, serverTime — no bulk create/update/delete. Docs: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_5130114613.html
Configuration.AsyncProcessingNote: Any REST request can be executed asynchronously by including the 'Prefer: respond-async' request header. The response includes a Location header pointing to the async job status URL: https://{accountId}.suitetalk.api.netsuite.com/services/rest/async/v1/job/{id}. Request size limit: 104MB. Source: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_161251386312.html

ErrorResponseShape.description: NetSuite REST API error responses use RFC 7807-style JSON with 'type' (URI), 'title' (human-readable), 'status' (HTTP status code), and 'o:errorDetails' array. Each error detail has 'detail' (human-readable), 'o:errorCode' (machine-readable error category), and optional 'o:errorPath' (JSON path where error occurred), 'o:urlPath', 'o:errorHeader', 'o:errorQueryParam'. Source: Error Handling in REST Web Services (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_156570709583.html).

title.description: HTTP status name (e.g. 'Unauthorized', 'Bad Request', 'Not Found', 'Internal Server Error')

o:errorCode.description: Machine-readable error category (e.g. INVALID_LOGIN, INVALID_CONTENT, NONEXISTENT_ID, CONCURRENCY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED, INVALID_REQUEST)

o:errorPath.description: JSON path in request body where error occurred

o:urlPath.description: URI path component where error occurred

ErrorResponseShape.rateLimitDetail: Concurrent request limit exceeded. Request blocked.

Configuration.CustomObjectMarkerPatternNote: Custom record types in NetSuite are accessible via the REST API as 'customrecord_{scriptId}' (the script ID assigned when the record was created, e.g. 'customrecord_project_task'). Custom records appear in the metadata catalog the same as standard records. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide 'Working with Resource Metadata': 'Custom record metadata is accessible the same way as metadata for standard records. As new customizations are added to the system, they appear in the metadata catalog as well.' and 'Working with Records': 'Using CRUD Operations on Custom Records, Custom Lists, and Custom Transactions v1'. EvidenceStrength: ImpliedFromExample (the pattern 'customrecord_' is the standard NetSuite script ID prefix for custom records).
Configuration.CustomFieldMarkerPatternNote: Custom fields in NetSuite are identified by their scriptId prefix based on type: custentity_ (entity custom fields on Customer, Contact, etc.), custbody_ (transaction body fields), custcolumn_ (transaction line item fields), custitem_ (item custom fields). In JSON Schema metadata, custom fields carry 'x-ns-custom-field: true'. Source: Working with JSON Schema Metadata example (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_1545129726.html): '"custentity_integer": { "title": "Integer Number", "type": "integer", ..., "x-ns-custom-field": true }'. EvidenceStrength: ImpliedFromExample.

WriteCapability.note: Full CRUD is supported for all record types that support the respective operations. The metadata catalog (OpenAPI 3.0) documents which HTTP methods (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) are available per record type. PATCH is used for partial updates. An upsert (PATCH with externalId) is also supported. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide 'Working with Records': 'Using REST web services, you can perform CRUD (create, read, update, delete) operations on NetSuite records.' and chapter TOC listing 'Creating a Record Instance', 'Getting a Record Instance', 'Updating a Record Instance', 'Using the Upsert Operation', 'Deleting a Record Instance'. URL: https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_1540811107.html

Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNote: NetSuite REST Web Services does not document ETags, If-Match headers, or version fields for optimistic concurrency control on individual record operations. PATCH operations update the record without conditional headers. The docuement mentions 'Using the Upsert Operation' but no concurrency mechanism. Conflict resolution must use application-level snapshot comparison (MostRecent / DestWins logic). EvidenceStrength: ImpliedFromExample (ETags/If-Match are absent from all documented request/response examples and the error code list).
Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNote: DELETE on /services/rest/record/v1/{recordType}/{id} permanently removes the record. NetSuite does have an 'isInactive' boolean field on many record types for logical deactivation (equivalent to a soft delete at the application level), but the REST DELETE verb performs hard deletion. The connector must handle both scenarios: records soft-deleted via isInactive=true will still appear in SuiteQL queries unless filtered. Source: SuiteTalk REST API Guide 'Deleting a Record Instance' listed in the Working with Records chapter (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_1540811107.html). EvidenceStrength: ImpliedFromExample (DELETE is a standard REST verb listed without any archival-flag qualifier in the CRUD docs).
Configuration.DefaultSyncDirectionNote: Read-only (Pull) is the safe default for new EntityMaps. NetSuite is an ERP system where incorrect writes can affect accounting records, financial transactions, and multi-subsidiary operations. Bidirectional must be validated with the client against a sandbox account before enabling, and write operations tested per-record-type. The client must confirm which record types should be bidirectional.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionNote: Recommended DestWins (NetSuite wins) for bidirectional mode because there is no ETag/version field (ConcurrencyControl=none), making MostRecent unreliable without additional infrastructure. If the client's external system must win, SourceWins is acceptable but requires explicit client opt-in and validation against their specific NetSuite setup.
Configuration.ScopeReason: Operator-specified knowingly: SuiteTalk REST Web Services only — Record Service (CRUD on standard + custom record types) + SuiteQL query service. SuiteAnalytics Connect excluded (JDBC/ODBC protocol, not REST/JSON). SuiteTalk SOAP excluded (legacy XML, being deprecated — last endpoint 2025.2, disabled 2028.2). RESTlets excluded (customer-specific SuiteScript, no standard schema).
Configuration.SOAPDeprecationNote: Oracle NetSuite is deprecating SuiteTalk SOAP Web Services. According to Oracle's deprecation policy, the SOAP endpoint will be disabled in a future release (timeline: last endpoint supported through 2025.2, planned disablement 2028.2). The REST API Guide includes a 'SOAP Web Services To REST Web Services Upgrade Guide' as the authoritative migration path. This connector targets the REST API exclusively. Source: SuiteTalk Web Services landing page (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/set_22152129.html) listing 'SOAP Web Services To Rest Web Services Upgrade Guide' as a primary resource.
Configuration.TokenRefreshStrategyNote: NetSuite TBA (OAuth 1.0a) tokens do NOT expire by default — they are long-lived until explicitly revoked by an administrator. The connector does not need to refresh tokens. If OAuth 2.0 is adopted as the auth method, standard OAuth 2.0 access token refresh applies (implementation needed at that time). Source: NetSuite TBA documentation implies persistent tokens (Access Token ID/Secret issued once and reused). EvidenceStrength: ImpliedFromExample.
Configuration.RecordsBodyFormatNote: NetSuite REST uses custom media types for request/response bodies. The standard content type for record operations is 'application/vnd.oracle.resource+json' with a type parameter: 'type=collection' (list), 'type=singular' (single record), 'type=create-form', 'type=edit-form'. Standard 'application/json' is used for error responses and SuiteQL bodies. Source: Working with OpenAPI 3.0 Metadata response excerpt showing content types; create-form operation showing Accept/Content-Type header values. https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/article_1217103046.html
Configuration.SuiteQLRequiredHeaderNote: SuiteQL POST requests MUST include the header 'Prefer: transient'. Without this header, the request will fail. Source: Executing SuiteQL Queries Through REST Web Services (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/section_157909186990.html): 'Note that Prefer: transient is a required header parameter.'
Configuration.RequestSizeLimitNote: REST requests have a maximum size of 104MB. Source: REST Web Services Request Processing (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/chapter_161251386312.html): 'Note: REST requests have a size limit of 104MB.'

SublistScopeExtension.decision: Retain 2 representative sublist child IOs (InvoiceItem, SalesOrderItem) as Declared, read-only, graph-descended objects that sit OUTSIDE the 203-type Oracle authoritative REST catalog.
SublistScopeExtension.accessPathEvidence: SuiteTalk REST API Guide 'Working with Sublists' — sublist access paths GET /services/rest/record/v1/{recordType}/{id}/{sublist} are Tier-1 documented (https://docs.oracle.com/en/cloud/saas/netsuite/ns-online-help/book_1559132836.html).
SublistScopeExtension.schemaEvidence: Specific line-item field schemas (item/quantity/rate/amount) sourced from community_openapi_2024_2.yaml (Tier-3, low confidence). NOT used for any hard constraint.
SublistScopeExtension.hardConstraintPosture: All Tier-3-only hard constraints on the FK fields (IsRequired, IsReadOnly) downgraded to false; AllowsNull set null (no source opinion). The FK edge itself is Tier-1 structural (parametric-child nesting), so IsForeignKey + RelatedIntegrationObjectID are retained.
SublistScopeExtension.syncPosture: SupportsWrite=false, SupportsIncrementalSync=false, no incremental watermark — these IOs demonstrate the sublist access-path pattern only.

Configuration.suiteQLReadStrategy: SELECT * FROM event WHERE lastModifiedDate > :watermark ORDER BY lastModifiedDate

fields.Name: id
fields.Description: NetSuite internal record id. PK proven by the addressing path GET /services/rest/record/v1/event/{id} — the API cannot address a single record without it (Tier-1 structural PK proof). Vendor-wide universal id convention.

fields.Name: lastModifiedDate
fields.Description: System last-modified timestamp used as the SuiteQL incremental watermark (SELECT ... WHERE lastModifiedDate > :watermark). Source: SuiteTalk serverTime + SuiteQL incremental docs. Per-type presence confirmed at runtime via metadata-catalog.
